questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A Sankey diagram shows a wide useful arrow and a narrow wasted arrow. What does this mean?
options: The device has zero energy input.
The device destroys most of the energy.
The device does no useful work.
questions.explanation: A larger useful output compared with wasted output means a higher efficiency.
questions.senNote: A larger useful output compared with wasted output means a higher efficiency.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: When a ball falls, which energy store decreases most clearly?
questions.explanation: As a ball falls, gravitational potential energy decreases and kinetic energy usually increases.
questions.senNote: As a ball falls, gravitational potential energy decreases and kinetic energy usually increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain how insulation reduces energy transfer
questions.question: Why does thicker loft insulation reduce energy transfer from a house?
options: It increases the temperature difference across the roof.
It creates more energy inside the roof.
It slows the rate of thermal energy transfer by conduction.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows the rate of thermal energy transfer by conduction.
questions.explanation: Insulation traps air and reduces conduction, which slows thermal energy transfer.
questions.senNote: Insulation traps air and reduces conduction, which slows thermal energy transfer.

questions.senNote: Insulation traps air and reduces conduction, which slows thermal energy transfer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A 3 kg trolley moves at 3 m/s. What is its kinetic energy?
questions.explanation: Kinetic energy equals 0.5 x mass x speed squared.
questions.senNote: Kinetic energy equals 0.5 x mass x speed squared.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Calculate power from energy and time
questions.question: An electric heater transfers 650 J of energy in 25 s. What is its power?
questions.explanation: Power equals energy transferred divided by time taken.
questions.senNote: Power equals energy transferred divided by time taken.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Calculate power from energy and time
questions.question: An electric heater transfers 700 J of energy in 30 s. What is its power?
questions.explanation: Power equals energy transferred divided by time taken.

questions.senNote: Power equals energy transferred divided by time taken.
